title: Mixed-Subjects IRT Calibration
text: example uses simulated LLM responses vignette can run without external API calls.
code: library(mixedsubjectsirt) library(ggplot2)  set.seed(2026)  n_human <- 400 n_generated <- 1200 n_items <- 8  true_pars <- data.frame(   item = paste0("Item", seq_len(n_items)),   a = seq(0.8, 1.6, length.out = n_items),   d = seq(-1.1, 1.1, length.out = n_items) ) true_pars$b <- -true_pars$d / true_pars$a  theta_human <- rnorm(n_human) observed <- simulate_2pl(theta_human, true_pars)  llm_pars <- true_pars llm_pars$a <- pmax(0.4, 0.9 * true_pars$a + rnorm(n_items, 0, 0.05)) llm_pars$d <- true_pars$d + 0.25 + rnorm(n_items, 0, 0.15) llm_pars$b <- -llm_pars$d / llm_pars$a  predicted <- simulate_2pl(theta_human, llm_pars) generated <- simulate_2pl(rnorm(n_generated, mean = 0.1, sd = 1.05), llm_pars)

title: Mixed-Subjects IRT Calibration
text: -split workflow uses human responses construct human posterior weights uses posterior weights paired LLM responses. default controlled common_predicted_weights = TRUE. fitted item parameters returned slope-intercept form.
code: human_start <- fit_2pl(   observed,   technical = list(NCYCLES = 500) )  human_only <- fit_mixed_subjects(   observed = observed,   predicted = predicted,   generated = generated,   lambda = 0,   initial_pars = human_start$pars,   n_quad = 11 )  mixed_no_split <- fit_mixed_subjects(   observed = observed,   predicted = predicted,   generated = generated,   lambda = 0.5,   initial_pars = human_start$pars,   n_quad = 11 )  mixed_no_split #> mixedsubjectsirt 2PL fit #>   items:      8 #>   lambda:     0.5 #>   loss:       3.46656 #>   convergence: 0 head(mixed_no_split$item_pars) #>    item        a           d            b #> 1 Item1 0.834782 -1.26456880  1.514849150 #> 2 Item2 1.569111 -1.16975103  0.745486595 #> 3 Item3 1.233131 -0.20684245  0.167737650 #> 4 Item4 1.317618 -0.49182798  0.373270577 #> 5 Item5 4.663752 -0.02325231  0.004985752 #> 6 Item6 2.166513  0.57443514 -0.265142668

title: Mixed-Subjects IRT Calibration
text: split workflow cross-fits posterior weights used labeled human paired LLM terms. split, initial human 2PL model estimated splits, used summarize held-split. Every human row still contributes final estimating equation .
code: mixed_split <- fit_mixed_subjects_split(   observed = observed,   predicted = predicted,   generated = generated,   lambda = 0.5,   n_splits = 2,   seed = 2026,   n_quad = 11,   technical = list(NCYCLES = 500) )  mixed_split #> mixedsubjectsirt 2PL fit #>   items:      8 #>   lambda:     0.5 #>   loss:       -6.05218e+23 #>   convergence: 0  #>   splits:     2

title: Mixed-Subjects IRT Calibration
text: real calibration study true item parameters unknown. simulation useful checking workflow behaving expected.  helper tune_lambda_grid() can used quick sensitivity checks candidate lambda values. summary reports optimized mixed objective human expected-count loss, final lambda selection tied study’s inferential target evaluated bootstrap/cross-validation plan.
code: estimates <- rbind(   data.frame(estimator = "human only", human_only$item_pars),   data.frame(estimator = "mixed no split", mixed_no_split$item_pars),   data.frame(estimator = "mixed split", mixed_split$item_pars) ) estimates$true_b <- true_pars$b[match(estimates$item, true_pars$item)] estimates$true_a <- true_pars$a[match(estimates$item, true_pars$item)]  ggplot(estimates, aes(true_b, b, color = estimator)) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   labs(x = "True difficulty", y = "Estimated difficulty", color = NULL) +   theme_minimal() tuned <- tune_lambda_grid(   lambda_grid = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75),   observed = observed,   predicted = predicted,   generated = generated,   initial_pars = human_start$pars,   n_quad = 11,   control = list(maxit = 200) )  tuned$summary #>   lambda    mixed_loss observed_loss convergence #> 1   0.00  4.081765e+00  4.081765e+00           0 #> 2   0.25  3.825618e+00  4.117991e+00           0 #> 3   0.50  3.466565e+00  4.459876e+00           0 #> 4   0.75 -3.167271e+36  1.168082e+37          52

what: Item Response Theory Calibration with a Mixed Subjects Design
title: Item Response Theory Calibration with a Mixed Subjects Design
text: mixedsubjectsirt package allows integration LLM-generated item responses psychometric calibration studies.

title: Item Response Theory Calibration with a Mixed Subjects Design
text: can install development version mixedsubjectsirt like :
code: devtools::install_github('klintkanopka/mixedsubjectsirt')

what: Fit a unidimensional 2PL IRT model — fit_2pl
title: Fit a unidimensional 2PL IRT model — fit_2pl
text: Fits two-parameter logistic model mirt returns item parameters slope-intercept form. response probability plogis(d + * theta), discrimination d intercept. Difficulty returned b = -d / .

title: Fit a unidimensional 2PL IRT model — fit_2pl
code: fit_2pl(resp, technical = list(NCYCLES = 1000), verbose = FALSE, ...)

title: Fit a unidimensional 2PL IRT model — fit_2pl
text: resp numeric item response matrix rows subjects columns items. Values must binary 0/1; NA allowed. technical list passed technical argument mirt::mirt(). verbose Logical; passed mirt::mirt(). ... Additional arguments passed mirt::mirt().

title: Fit a unidimensional 2PL IRT model — fit_2pl
text: list pars, data frame containing item, , d, b, model, fitted mirt model.

title: Fit a unidimensional 2PL IRT model — fit_2pl
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- fit_2pl(response_matrix) fit$pars } # }

title: Fit a mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects
text: Fits item parameters using observed human responses, paired LLM responses/predictions subjects, generated unlabeled LLM responses. implements expected-count objective

title: Fit a mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects
code: fit_mixed_subjects(   observed,   predicted,   generated,   lambda = 1,   n_quad = 31,   initial_pars = NULL,   quadrature = NULL,   common_predicted_weights = TRUE,   slope_lower = 1e-04,   control = list(maxit = 500),   ... )

title: Fit a mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects
text: observed Human response matrix, rows subjects columns items. Values must binary initial_pars omitted. predicted LLM responses probabilities rows items observed. generated Generated unlabeled LLM responses probabilities item columns. lambda Power-tuning parameter [0, 1]. n_quad Number standard-normal quadrature nodes. initial_pars Optional starting item parameters. omitted, 2PL model fit observed. quadrature Optional quadrature grid theta weight columns. common_predicted_weights Logical; TRUE, reuse observed human posterior weights predicted. slope_lower Lower bound discrimination parameters optimization. Use NULL lower bound. control Control list passed stats::optim(). ... Additional arguments passed fit_2pl() initial_pars omitted.

title: Fit a mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects
text: object class "mixedsubjects_fit" fitted item_pars, optimizer details, quadrature summaries, input settings.

title: Fit a mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects
text: L_human + lambda * (L_generated - L_paired_llm). default paired LLM responses reuse posterior quadrature weights observed human responses. keeps paired human LLM terms latent covariate distribution, closest analog prediction-powered inference paired labels.

title: Fit a mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects
code: set.seed(1) pars <- data.frame(a = c(1, 1.2, 0.9), d = c(0, -0.5, 0.3)) observed <- simulate_2pl(rnorm(40), pars) predicted <- observed generated <- simulate_2pl(rnorm(100), pars) fit <- fit_mixed_subjects(   observed, predicted, generated,   lambda = 0.5, initial_pars = pars, n_quad = 7,   control = list(maxit = 50) ) fit$item_pars #>   item         a           d           b #> 1    1 0.9345154 -0.05509868  0.05895963 #> 2    2 1.0991610 -0.84500017  0.76876835 #> 3    3 0.7051842  0.24631388 -0.34929016

what: Fit a split-sample mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects_split
title: Fit a split-sample mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects_split
text: Fits objective fit_mixed_subjects(), constructs labeled expected counts cross-fitted posterior weights. split, initial human 2PL model fit splits used compute posterior weights held-split. human row contributes final estimating equation exactly .

title: Fit a split-sample mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects_split
code: fit_mixed_subjects_split(   observed,   predicted,   generated,   lambda = 1,   n_splits = 2,   split_id = NULL,   seed = NULL,   n_quad = 31,   initial_pars = NULL,   quadrature = NULL,   common_predicted_weights = TRUE,   slope_lower = 1e-04,   control = list(maxit = 500),   ... )

title: Fit a split-sample mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects_split
text: observed Human response matrix, rows subjects columns items. Values must binary initial_pars omitted. predicted LLM responses probabilities rows items observed. generated Generated unlabeled LLM responses probabilities item columns. lambda Power-tuning parameter [0, 1]. n_splits Number sample splits. split_id Optional integer vector assigning observed row split. omitted, splits sampled random. seed Optional random seed used split_id omitted. n_quad Number standard-normal quadrature nodes. initial_pars Optional item parameters use every fold instead fitting fold-specific human models. mainly useful testing sensitivity analyses. quadrature Optional quadrature grid theta weight columns. common_predicted_weights Logical; TRUE, reuse held-observed posterior weight matrix paired LLM responses. slope_lower Lower bound discrimination parameters optimization. Use NULL lower bound. control Control list passed stats::optim(). ... Additional arguments passed fit_2pl() fold-specific initial models fit.

title: Fit a split-sample mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects_split
text: object class "mixedsubjects_fit" split metadata fold-level initial parameters.

title: Fit a split-sample mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects_split
text: Generated LLM counts computed per fold averaged across folds generated sample keeps original sample-size scale.

title: Fit a split-sample mixed-subjects 2PL calibration — fit_mixed_subjects_split
code: set.seed(2) pars <- data.frame(a = c(1, 1.2, 0.9), d = c(0, -0.5, 0.3)) observed <- simulate_2pl(rnorm(40), pars) predicted <- observed generated <- simulate_2pl(rnorm(100), pars) fit <- fit_mixed_subjects_split(   observed, predicted, generated,   lambda = 0.5, initial_pars = pars, n_splits = 2,   n_quad = 7, control = list(maxit = 50) ) fit$item_pars #>   item         a          d          b #> 1    1 0.8474584  0.1057631 -0.1248004 #> 2    2 1.1092225 -0.6137239  0.5532919 #> 3    3 0.8812820  0.4579316 -0.5196198

what: Link item parameters onto a target scale — link_item_parameters
title: Link item parameters onto a target scale — link_item_parameters
text: Applies mean-mean linking express source item parameters scale target calibration. parameter sets must slope-intercept form model plogis(d + * theta).

title: Link item parameters onto a target scale — link_item_parameters
code: link_item_parameters(source, target, method = c("mean_mean", "none"))

title: Link item parameters onto a target scale — link_item_parameters
text: source Item parameters transform. matrix data frame columns /a1 d, fitted mirt model. target Item parameters defining target scale. Uses accepted formats source. method Linking method. Currently "mean_mean" "none" supported.

title: Link item parameters onto a target scale — link_item_parameters
text: list transformed pars, linking constants B, selected method.

title: Link item parameters onto a target scale — link_item_parameters
text: theta_target = * theta_source + B, source parameters transform a_target = a_source / b_target = * b_source + B, d_target = -a_target * b_target. Mean-mean linking chooses B transformed source parameters match target mean discrimination mean difficulty.

title: Link item parameters onto a target scale — link_item_parameters
code: source <- data.frame(a = c(0.8, 1.2), d = c(-0.2, 0.5)) target <- data.frame(a = c(1.0, 1.5), d = c(-0.1, 0.4)) link_item_parameters(source, target)$pars #>   item   a          d          b #> 1    1 1.0 -0.1833333  0.1833333 #> 2    2 1.5  0.5250000 -0.3500000

what: Create a standard-normal Gauss-Hermite quadrature grid — make_quadrature
title: Create a standard-normal Gauss-Hermite quadrature grid — make_quadrature
text: rmutil::gauss.hermite() returns nodes weights integrals form integral f(x) exp(-x^2) dx. function rescales nodes weights approximate expectations standard normal latent trait distribution.

title: Create a standard-normal Gauss-Hermite quadrature grid — make_quadrature
code: make_quadrature(n_quad = 31, iterlim = 1e+05)

title: Create a standard-normal Gauss-Hermite quadrature grid — make_quadrature
text: n_quad Number quadrature nodes. iterlim Maximum number Newton-Raphson iterations passed rmutil::gauss.hermite().

title: Create a standard-normal Gauss-Hermite quadrature grid — make_quadrature
text: data frame node index, theta, weight, backward compatible aliases X_k A_k.

title: Create a standard-normal Gauss-Hermite quadrature grid — make_quadrature
code: quad <- make_quadrature(7) sum(quad$weight) #> [1] 1

title: Mixed-subjects objective function — mixed_subjects_loss
text: Evaluates rectified mixed-subjects loss 2PL item parameters. parameter vector must contain discriminations first, followed intercepts. response probability plogis(d + * theta).

title: Mixed-subjects objective function — mixed_subjects_loss
code: mixed_subjects_loss(pars, q_observed, q_predicted, q_llm, lambda = 0)

title: Mixed-subjects objective function — mixed_subjects_loss
text: pars Numeric vector item parameters: discriminations followed intercepts d. q_observed Quadrature summary observed human responses, usually returned mixed_subjects_quadrature(). q_predicted Quadrature summary LLM responses/predictions labeled human subjects. q_llm Quadrature summary generated unlabeled LLM responses. lambda Power-tuning parameter [0, 1].

title: Mixed-subjects objective function — mixed_subjects_loss
text: scalar loss.

title: Mixed-subjects objective function — mixed_subjects_loss
text: objective L_observed(pars) + lambda * (L_generated(pars) - L_predicted(pars)). Setting lambda = 0 gives human-expected-count objective.

title: Mixed-subjects objective function — mixed_subjects_loss
code: pars <- data.frame(a = c(1, 1.2), d = c(0, -0.5)) resp <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 1), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) q <- mixed_subjects_quadrature(resp, item_pars = pars, N_quad = 5) mixed_subjects_loss(c(pars$a, pars$d), q, q, q, lambda = 0.5) #> [1] 1.639302

what: Convert responses to quadrature form — mixed_subjects_quadrature
title: Convert responses to quadrature form — mixed_subjects_quadrature
text: Fits accepts 2PL model, computes posterior quadrature weights subject, returns expected counts mixed-subjects calibration. lower-level helper; analyses call fit_mixed_subjects() fit_mixed_subjects_split().

title: Convert responses to quadrature form — mixed_subjects_quadrature
code: mixed_subjects_quadrature(   resp,   N_quad = 31,   eps = 1e-15,   iterlim = 1e+05,   irt_pars = NULL,   item_pars = NULL,   quadrature = NULL,   link_method = "mean_mean",   ... )

title: Convert responses to quadrature form — mixed_subjects_quadrature
text: resp response matrix rows subjects columns items. N_quad Number quadrature nodes compute. Kept backward compatibility; prefer n_quad new code. eps Retained backward compatibility. Stable log computations used instead probability clipping. iterlim Maximum number Newton-Raphson iterations passed rmutil::gauss.hermite(). irt_pars Optional target item parameters mean-mean linking. argument kept backward compatibility earlier package versions. item_pars Optional item parameters. omitted, 2PL model fit resp using fit_2pl(). quadrature Optional quadrature grid theta weight columns. link_method Linking method used irt_pars supplied. ... Additional arguments passed fit_2pl() item_pars omitted.

title: Convert responses to quadrature form — mixed_subjects_quadrature
text: list quad, counts, weights, irt_pars, quadrature, theta.

title: Convert responses to quadrature form — mixed_subjects_quadrature
code: pars <- data.frame(a = c(1, 1.2), d = c(0, -0.5)) resp <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 1), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) q <- mixed_subjects_quadrature(resp, item_pars = pars, N_quad = 5) names(q) #> [1] "quad"       "counts"     "weights"    "irt_pars"   "quadrature" #> [6] "theta"

what: Compute posterior quadrature weights for a 2PL model — posterior_weights_2pl
title: Compute posterior quadrature weights for a 2PL model — posterior_weights_2pl
text: Computes subject's posterior distribution fixed quadrature grid 2PL model, using stable log-likelihood calculations. Fractional responses [0, 1] allowed, useful LLM output stored probabilities rather sampled binary responses.

title: Compute posterior quadrature weights for a 2PL model — posterior_weights_2pl
code: posterior_weights_2pl(   resp,   item_pars,   quadrature = NULL,   n_quad = 31,   iterlim = 1e+05 )

title: Compute posterior quadrature weights for a 2PL model — posterior_weights_2pl
text: resp response matrix rows subjects columns items. Values may binary, fractional [0, 1], NA. item_pars Item parameters slope-intercept form. Supply data frame matrix columns /a1 d, fitted mirt model. quadrature Optional quadrature data frame theta weight columns. omitted, standard-normal grid created. n_quad Number quadrature nodes used quadrature omitted. iterlim Maximum number Newton-Raphson iterations passed rmutil::gauss.hermite() quadrature omitted.

title: Compute posterior quadrature weights for a 2PL model — posterior_weights_2pl
text: matrix one row per subject one column per quadrature node. Rows sum one. Attributes theta weight contain grid.

title: Compute posterior quadrature weights for a 2PL model — posterior_weights_2pl
code: pars <- data.frame(a = c(1, 1.2), d = c(0, -0.5)) resp <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 1), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) W <- posterior_weights_2pl(resp, pars, n_quad = 5) rowSums(W) #> [1] 1 1

what: Estimate ability scores from a 2PL calibration — score_theta
title: Estimate ability scores from a 2PL calibration — score_theta
text: Computes bounded maximum-likelihood ability estimates response patterns fixed item parameters. scoring helper inspecting fitted calibrations; account uncertainty item parameters.

title: Estimate ability scores from a 2PL calibration — score_theta
code: score_theta(resp, item_pars, bounds = c(-6, 6))

title: Estimate ability scores from a 2PL calibration — score_theta
text: resp Response matrix rows subjects columns items. item_pars Item parameters slope-intercept form. Supply data frame matrix columns /a1 d, fitted mirt model. bounds Numeric vector length two giving optimization interval theta.

title: Estimate ability scores from a 2PL calibration — score_theta
text: numeric vector ability estimates.

title: Estimate ability scores from a 2PL calibration — score_theta
code: set.seed(1) pars <- data.frame(a = c(1, 1.2), d = c(0, -0.5)) resp <- simulate_2pl(rnorm(5), pars) score_theta(resp, pars) #> [1] -5.9999390  5.9999241  0.4122068  5.9999241  0.4122068

title: Simulate 2PL item responses — simulate_2pl
text: Generates binary item responses model plogis(d + * theta).

title: Simulate 2PL item responses — simulate_2pl
code: simulate_2pl(theta, item_pars)

title: Simulate 2PL item responses — simulate_2pl
text: theta Numeric vector latent trait values. item_pars Item parameters slope-intercept form. Supply data frame matrix columns /a1 d, fitted mirt model.

title: Simulate 2PL item responses — simulate_2pl
text: binary response matrix one row per value theta one column per item.

title: Simulate 2PL item responses — simulate_2pl

what: Summarize response data as expected quadrature counts — summarize_expected_counts
title: Summarize response data as expected quadrature counts — summarize_expected_counts
text: Converts response data posterior quadrature weights Bock-Aitkin style expected counts. item quadrature node, N expected number observed responses R expected number correct.

title: Summarize response data as expected quadrature counts — summarize_expected_counts
code: summarize_expected_counts(resp, weights)

title: Summarize response data as expected quadrature counts — summarize_expected_counts
text: resp response matrix rows subjects columns items. weights Posterior quadrature weights, usually returned posterior_weights_2pl().

title: Summarize response data as expected quadrature counts — summarize_expected_counts
text: list class "mixedsubjects_counts" containing matrices N R, sample size n, quadrature nodes, quadrature weights, item names.

title: Summarize response data as expected quadrature counts — summarize_expected_counts
code: pars <- data.frame(a = c(1, 1.2), d = c(0, -0.5)) resp <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 1), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) W <- posterior_weights_2pl(resp, pars, n_quad = 5) counts <- summarize_expected_counts(resp, W) counts$N #>             node1    node2    node3     node4       node5 #> Item1 0.001261709 0.202937 1.490278 0.3034757 0.002048037 #> Item2 0.001261709 0.202937 1.490278 0.3034757 0.002048037

what: Tune lambda over a grid — tune_lambda_grid
title: Tune lambda over a grid — tune_lambda_grid
text: Fits fit_mixed_subjects() fit_mixed_subjects_split() set candidate lambda values. returned summary reports fitted mixed-subjects objective observed human expected-count loss candidate. diagnostics replacement study-specific validation bootstrap procedure, useful sensitivity checks.

title: Tune lambda over a grid — tune_lambda_grid
code: tune_lambda_grid(   lambda_grid,   observed,   predicted,   generated,   split = FALSE,   ... )

title: Tune lambda over a grid — tune_lambda_grid
text: lambda_grid Numeric vector lambda values [0, 1]. observed, predicted, generated Response matrices passed fit_mixed_subjects(). split Logical; TRUE, call fit_mixed_subjects_split(). ... Additional arguments passed selected fitting function.

title: Tune lambda over a grid — tune_lambda_grid
text: list summary, best_lambda_by_observed_loss, fitted model objects.

title: Tune lambda over a grid — tune_lambda_grid
code: set.seed(3) pars <- data.frame(a = c(1, 1.2, 0.9), d = c(0, -0.5, 0.3)) observed <- simulate_2pl(rnorm(30), pars) predicted <- observed generated <- simulate_2pl(rnorm(80), pars) tuned <- tune_lambda_grid(   c(0, 0.5),   observed, predicted, generated,   initial_pars = pars, n_quad = 5, control = list(maxit = 30) ) tuned$summary #>   lambda mixed_loss observed_loss convergence #> 1    0.0   1.570232      1.570232           0 #> 2    0.5   1.721877      1.599580           0
